original: Valentinian III is elevated to Roman Emperor, at the age of 6
The Synodus Palmaris, called by Gothic King Theodoric the Great, discharges Pope Symmachus of all charges, thus ending the schism of Antipope Laurentius
Burgundy king Guntram opens synod of Mâcon (Mastico)
Byzantine empress Irene recovers Iconoclastic cult at Nicaea
Battle of az-Zallaqah: Almoravid army of Yusuf ibn Tashfin defeats the forces of Castilian King Alfonso VI
Tornado (possible T8/F4) strikes the heart of London killing two and demolishing the wooden London Bridge (OS 17 Oct)
Battle of Grathe Heath ends the civil war in Denmark. King Sweyn III is killed and Valdemar I restores the country.
Otto II becomes earl of Gelre
Remnants of Medina Sidonia's Spanish Armada returns to Santander, Spain
Outbreak of the Irish Rebellion of 1641 - Catholic uprising in Ulster
Sea battle of Fehmarn Sont: Adm Thijssen beats Denen
Jews of Barbados forbidden to engage in retail trade
Revolt in Haarlem after public ban on smoking
American colonial forces, led by Sir William Phipps, fail to seize Quebec
Siege of Spanish-held city of Ceuta on the north African coast begun by Moroccan forces - will be the longest siege in history lasting 26 years
Battle of Bay of Vigo: Dutch & English fleet destroy & occupy Spanish silver fleet & French squadron
First Jewish prayer books printed in North America
Slaves revolt in Haiti (later suppressed)
Sailing ship "Aeneus" sinks off Newfoundland killing 340

The Pacific Fur Company trading post in Astoria, Oregon is turned over to the rival British North West Company (the fur trade in the Pacific Northwest was dominated for the next three decades by the United Kingdom).
One of the first modern plastic surgeries in the west performed by Joseph Carpue on a soldier's nose in England using Indian techniques
1st ship sails through the Erie Canal from Rome, New York to Utica, New York
First US National Women's Rights convention opens in Brinley Hall, Worcester, Massachusetts
Maastricht-Aken railway in Netherlands opens
English newspaper "The Times" gives precise British positions in Crimea during Crimean War
Battle of Westport, Missouri: Union General Samuel R Curtis defeats Confederate General Stirling Price
72 Senators are summoned by Royal Proclamation to serve as the first members of the Canadian Senate.
Replacement yacht Sappho (US) beats Livonia (UK) by 25:27 in race 5 to win 3rd America's Cup off Newport, RI 4-1; original defender Columbia damaged so misses races 4 & 5
New Orleans Mint reopens as an assay office
Pelham Bay Park in Bronx vested
C. Dazey's "In Old Kentucky" premieres in NYC (27 seasons)
Edward Milton Royle's play "Squaw Man" premieres in NYC
Blanche Scott becomes the first woman to fly at a public event in the US at Fort Wayne, Indiana
Ritz Hotel in Madrid opens with 200 chambers and 100 bathtubs
1st national horseshoe throwing championship at Kellerton, Iowa
1st Infantry division "Big Red One" shoots 1st US shot in WW I

Orchestra Hall, designed by C. Howard Crane opens in Detroit, Michigan; home of the Detroit Symphony, 1919-39 and 1989 to present, also known as The Paradise Theater, featuring top jazz performers and films, 1941-51
Romberg & Atteridge's musical "Passing Show" premieres in NYC
African demonstrators shot in Port Elizabeth, South Africa
Green Bay Packers play 1st APFA (forerunner to NFL) game; beat Minneapolis Marines, 7-6 at Hagemeister Park, Green Bay, WI
Channing Pollock's play "Fool" premieres in NYC
US patent is issued to American inventor Sebastian Hinton for playground climbing structure popularly known as 'monkey bars' (No, 1471465)
Town of Netanya, Israel founded by Nathan Strauss
Jean Piccard & Jeanette Ridlen attain record balloon height of 10.9 miles (17.5 km) over Lake Erie

American mobstersDutch Schultz, Abe Landau, Otto Berman, and Bernard "Lulu" Rosencrantz are fatally shot by organized crime hitmen at a saloon in Newark, New Jersey, what becomes known as "The Chophouse Massacre" to prevent them from killing the US Attorney prosecuting Schultz for tax evasion, what becomes known as "The Chophouse Massacre"
Chicago Cubs catcherGabby Hartnettselected NL MVP

All 12 passengers and crewmen aboard an American Airlines DC-3 airliner killed when it is struck by a U.S. Army Air Forces bomber near Palm Springs, California. Amongst the victims is composer Ralph Rainger ("Thanks for the Memory")
During WWII, Britain launches major offensive at El Alamein, Egypt
First ships of invasion fleet to Morocco leave Norfolk
German units go through Red October factory in Stalingrad
First Jewish transport out of Rome reaches extermination camp Birkenau
Battle of Gulf of Leyte begins - largest naval battle of WWII near the Philippines
First Central Kitchen opens in Amsterdam
Soviet army invades Hungary
Vice Admiral Takeo Kurita's flagship the heavy cruiser Atago sinks during the Battle of Leyte Gulf
UN General Assembly 2nd session convenes (1st NYC-Flushing Meadows)

Husband & wife Carl Cori & Gerty Cori are 1st spouses to be awarded joint Nobel Prizes for Physiology or Medicine for their discovery of the course of the catalytic conversion of glycogen" (alongside Bernardo Alberto Houssay)
NAACP petition on racism "An Appeal to the World" presented to UN
France grants Laos' sovereignty
German FR applies to NATO
WTRF TV channel 7 in Wheeling-Steubenville, WV (CBS) 1st broadcast
Britain, France & US agree to end occupation of Germany
Pakistan Governor-General Ghoelan Mohammed disbands parliament
WSAU TV channel 7 in Wausau, WI (CBS) begins broadcasting
Dominican Professional Baseball League moves to winter play for first time
First video recording on magnetic tape televised coast-to-coast
Thousands of Hungarians protest against the government and Soviet occupation. (The Hungarian Revolution is crushed on November 4).
First test firing of Vanguard satellite launch vehicle, TV-3
De Gaulle offers Algerians defiance "peace of the brave"

The Springhill Mine Bump - underground earthquake traps 174 miners in No. 2 colliery at Springhill, Nova Scotia, deepest coal mine in North America. By 1st November rescuers had dug out 100 victims, with death toll at 74.
Chinese troops move into India, 17 die
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
USAF Major Robert A Rushworth takes X-15 to 40,800m
WCIV TV channel 4 in Charleston, South Carolina (NBC) begins broadcasting
Czech gymnast Věra Čáslavská wins the balance beam at the Tokyo Olympics; her 3rd gold medal of the Games with individual all-round and vault victories
Hungary beats Czechoslovakia 2-1 to win the men's football gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics
Japan beats the Soviet Union 3-0 to claim the inaugural Olympic women's volleyball gold medal in Tokyo; undefeated in 6-team round robin competition
Japanese gymnast Yukio Endo wins the parallel bars gold medal in the Tokyo Olympics; his 3rd gold medal of the Games (individual all-round and team); 4th career gold (1960, 64)
Soviet Union and Czechoslovakia finish 8-1 in inaugural Olympic men's volleyball competition in Tokyo; Soviets win 10 team round robin on count back of sets won-lost
Time Magazine uses term "op art" for 1st time

Australian Jack Brabham is first to win F1 World Drivers Championship and International Cup for Constructors Championship in the same year; 2nd in season ending Mexican Grand Prix at Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
NJ Americans (later NY/NJ Nets) play 1st ABA game
American swimmer Kaye Hall sets a world record 1:06.2 to beat Canadian Elaine Tanner by 0.5s and win the 100m backstroke gold medal at the Mexico City Olympics
Charles Haughey and two others are found not guilty of illegal arms importation by a Dublin jury; the 'Arms Trial' began on 28 May 1970
Gary Gabelich sets auto speed record 622.4 mph (1,001 kph)
Three Catholic civilians are shot dead by the British Army during an attempted robbery in Newry, County Down
Two female members of the Irish Republican Army (IRA) are shot dead by the British Army in the Lower Falls area of Belfast
WXLT (now WWSB) TV channel 40 in Sarasota-Bradenton, FL (ABC) begins
Access credit cards introduced in Great Britain
Loyalist paramilitaries carry out raid on an Ulster Defence Regiment
WNJS TV channel 23 in Camden, NJ (PBS) begins broadcasting
Arab oil embargo extended to the Netherlands
UN's revised International Telecommunication Convention adopted
Yankee GM & President Lee MacPhail named AL president
Chicago Cubs trade 6-time MLB All Star outfielder Billy Williams to Oakland A's for second baseman Manny Trillo and 2 pitchers
Lake Isaac in Cleveland Metroparks' Big Creek Reservation dedicated
Battle between Cuba & South Africa troops in Angola
Islander Glenn Resch's 5th shut-out opponent-Flyers 3-0
Women take the day off in Iceland to commemorate “International Women’s Year”, shutting the country down for a day
Paleontologist Elso Barghoorn announces the discovery of a 3.4 billion-year-old single-celled fossil, one of the earliest life forms on Earth
Panamanians vote 2:1 to approve new Canal treaties
CBS raises LP prices to $8.98
China & Japan formally ends 4 decades of dissension
US national debt hits $1 trillion
400,000 demonstrate in Brussels, against cruise missile
Suicide terrorist truck bomb kills 243 US personnel in Beirut
Chicago Cubs Rick Sutcliffe, selected as a unanimous choice as NL Cy Young
NBC airs BBC footage of Ethiopian famine
STS 51-A launch vehicle moves to launch pad
Dutch government gives Fokker's Aircraft Ÿ212 million credit
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
US Senate rejects Robert Bork's nomination to the Supreme Court by a bipartisan vote of 58-42
Boston Celtics play Spain in Madrid
Browns' Bernie Kosar sets club record with a 97-yard TD pass
Hungary proclaims itself a republic & declares communist rule ended
US 62nd manned space mission STS 34 (Atlantis 5) returns from space
William Nicholson's "Shadowlands" premieres in London
Iraq announces release of 330 French hostages
Paramilitia kills 22 demonstrators at Bijbihara in Indian-controlled Kashmir
Seven people killed by IRA bomb attack in Belfast
"The MacNeil-Lehrer Report" becomes "The NewsHour with Jim Lehrer", upon Robert MacNeil's retirement (currently known as "PBS NewsHour") on PBS
"Triumph of Love" opens at Royale Theater NYC
At 6:11 AM San Francisco experiences a power blackout due to sabotage
Dow Jones Industrial Average drops 186.88 points
Les Alexander, owner of Houston Rockets buys NHL's Edmonton Oilers

"Monday Night Miracle", down 30-7 at the end of the third quarter, New York Jets pull together an improbable comeback with 4 touchdowns and a field goal in the fourth quarter, eventually defeating the Miami Dolphins 40-37 in overtime
Apple releases the iPod
Provisional Irish Republican Army of Northern Ireland commences disarmament following peace talks
Moscow Theatre Siege begins: Chechen rebels seize the House of Culture theater in Moscow and take approximately 700 theater-goers hostage
A powerful earthquake and its aftershocks hit Niigata prefecture, northern Japan, killing 35 people, injuring 2,200, and leaving 85,000 homeless or evacuated.
The UK markets had contracted by 0.4% in the third quarter against what was expected to be a period of growth, as a result of unexpectedly poor performance by the service sector
The United Nations "Rotterdam Rules" convention regulating international maritime carriage of goods is ratified with its twentieth signature.
7th Rugby World Cup Final, Eden Park, Auckland: Tournament favourites New Zealand edge France, 8-7; crowd 61,079
A powerful 7.2 magnitude earthquake strikes Van Province, Turkey, killing 582 people and injuring thousands.
12 people are killed and 40 are injured in a hospital fire in Tainan, Taiwan
European Commission rejects Italy's budget, first country to be rejected
Megyn Kelly is criticized after making comments supporting blackface on her NBC show
Microplastics found in human stools for the first time by Austrian scientists
World's oldest intact shipwreck, ancient Greek vessel 2,400 years old, found at bottom of the Black Sea by archaeologists
Google research lab claims it has achieved Quantum Supremacy, performing calculation in 3 mins that wold take a supercomputer 10,000 years
Hong Kong Legislative Council scraps the extradition bill that sparked months of protests and unrest
Lorry containing 39 bodies of Vietnamese nationals found in Essex, England, man arrested for people smuggling and murder
Capture of Colombia's most-wanted drug lord, Dairo Antonio Usuga 'Otoniel', in Colombia's Uraba region, announced live on TV
Xi Jinpingsecures record third term as general secretary of the Chinese Communist Party with a new seven-member Politburo Standing Committee, at the 20th National Party Congress in Beijing
'Superfog' conditions near New Orleans cause a motorway pile-up involving 158 vehicles, causing seven deaths
French woman Gisèle Pelicot speaks in court in Avignon against her husband, accused of drugging and arranging other men to rape her, saying "it's not for us to have shame - it's for them"
Tropical storm Trami / Kristine makes landfall in Divilacan, Philippines, creating severe flooding that kills at least 130 people
Roman Republican civil wars: SecondBattle of Philippi-Brutus's army is decisively defeated byMark AntonyandOctavian. Brutus commits suicide.
Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vcrowned King of Germany
Battle of Edgehill (Warwickshire): KingCharles Ibeat English parliamentarian forces
Meal Tub Plot against James Duke of York (futureJames IIof England)
English KingCharles IIrevokes the Massachusetts Bay Colony charter due to repeated violations of the charter’s terms including trading with other countries and running an illegal mint
War of Jenkins' Ear starts: British Prime Minister,Robert Walpole, reluctantly declares war on Spain
Failed coup against French EmperorNapoleon Bonaparte
Leos Janacek's opera "Kat'a Kabanova" premieres in Brno
Conservative AndrewBonar Lawforms government in United Kingdom, replacingDavid Lloyd George's Liberal government
Legendary Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthmakes a postseason exhibition appearance in a rival Giants uniform as NY beats Baltimore Orioles, 9-0 in a benefit game for former Giants owner John Day
Radio comedy program "TheFred AllenShow" premieres
John Dillingerand his gang rob Central National Bank, in Greencastle, Indiana. They take $75, 000.
Mackenzie Kingis elected as Prime Minister of Canada for the third time
Walt Disney's animated film "Dumbo" released
GeneralEnver Hoxhabecomes interim Prime Minister of Albania  - retains the leadership until his death in 1985
American baseball playerJackie Robinsonsigns contract with Montreal Royals, minor league farm team of Brooklyn Dodgers
Filipino PresidentElpidio Quirinoissues Executive Order No. 355 replacing the National Land Settlement Administration with Land Settlement Development Corporation (LASEDECO)
English chemistRosalind Franklinfirst identifies the two types of carbon produced by temperature, in paper published by the Royal Society

Charlie Chaplin's "Limelight", starring himself and Claire Bloom, with an appearance byBuster Keaton, premieres in New York City; Not released in Los Angeles until 1972, winning Chaplin his only competitive Academy Award for original score
Soviet novelistBoris Pasternak, wins Nobel Prize for Literature
Adlai Stevensonspeaks at the United Nations about the Cuba crisis
New York Yankees nameYogi Berraas manager, replacing Ralph Houk who becomes the team's general manager
Dutch 10th dan judokaAnton Geesinkwins Open gold medal in the first ever Olympic judo competition in Tokyo; prevents clean sweep of the gold medals by Japan
Future undisputed world heavyweight boxing championJoe Frazierdominates German Hans Huber for an easy points win and the Olympic heavyweight gold medal in Tokyo
Soviet gymnastLarisa Latyninawins the floor exercise gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics; her 2nd gold of the Games (team) and career 9th (1956, 1960, 1964), a gymnastics record
"I'm Still in Love with You" 5th studio album byAl Greenis released (Billboard Album of the Year 1973)
19-year-old American tennis starChris Evertretains her WTA Tour Championship at Boca Raton, Florida; beatsNancy RicheyGunter 6-3, 6-3 in the final
Richard Nixonagrees to turn over White House tape recordings to JudgeJohn Sirica
English rock starElton Johnreceives a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
Despite not driving in season ending Japanese Grand Prix, AustrianNiki Laudawins his second Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 17 points from Jody Scheckter of South Africa
Punk rock singerSid Viciousattempts suicide while at Riker's Detention Center in NYC
Nikolai Tikhonov succeedsAlexei Kosyginas Premier of the Soviet Union, due to illness
"Concerto for Orchestra" by American composerRoger Sessionspremieres with Seiji Ozawa conducting the Boston Symphony Orchestra
ArtistKeith Haringcommissioned to paint a mural on the Berlin wall by Checkpoint Charlie Museum 300 metres long
George Harrisonreleases "Best of Dark Horse 1976-89" album
Clarence Thomassworn in as US Supreme Court Justice
DrJack Kevorkian's suicide machine assists 2 women to commit suicide
EmperorAkihitobecomes the first Emperor of Japan to stand on Chinese soil

Israeli-Palestinian Conflict: Israeli Prime MinisterBenjamin Netanyahuand Palestinian ChairmanYasser Arafatagree to the Wye River Memorandum, resuming implementation of the stalled Oslo II Accord which established a level of self-government for Palestine; signed at the White House, In Washington D.C.
TheWalt DisneyConcert Hall, designed byFrank Gehryas "a living room for Los Angeles" opens with a gala
"Irreplaceable" single released by Beyonce (Soul Train Music Awards Best R&B/Soul Single 2007, Billboard Song of the Year 2007)
"The Black Parade" 3rd studio album byMy Chemical Romanceis released

Rounder Records releases "Raising Sand", a collaborative album by American bluegrass singerAlison Kraussand British rock vocalistRobert Plant; tops the chart in Norway, peaks at #2 in US, UK, and Sweden
Joe Sakicscores his final career goal (#625) against the Edmonton Oilers
"Skyfall", 23rd James Bond film, directed bySam Mendesand starringDaniel CraigandJavier Bardempremieres in London
Adelereleases her single "Hello," becomes the first song with more than a million downloads in its first week
Freddie Mercurybio film "Bohemian Rhapsody" directed by Bryan Singer, starringRami Malek(Best Actor Academy Awards 2019) premieres in London
Turkish President Recep TayyipErdoganrejects Saudi claim journalistJamal Khashoggikilled accidentally, says it was premeditated murder
World's longest sea-crossing bridge, the Hong Kong Macau Zhuhai bridge at 55km, opened by Chinese PresidentXi Jinping
All Star point guardKyrie Irvingpours in 50 points, setting a new NBA record for points on debut with a new team as his Brooklyn Nets go down 127-126 at home to the Minnesota Timberwolves
Columbia Records releasesBruce Springsteen's 20th studio album "Letter To You", recorded with the E Street Band
Netflix releases miniseries "The Queen's Gambit" starringAnya Taylor-Joy- 1st streaming show to win Emmy for Outstanding Limited or Anthology Series
Brock Purdyplays against the Kansas City Chiefs, throwing for 66 yards and an interception
Library of manuscript collector Robert Cotton is badly damaged in a fire in London, lost are one of only two surviving copies ofKing John's Magna Carta (1215) and Asser's biography of King Alfred
First aerial reconnaissance mission is flown by an Italian pilot over Turkish lines during the Italo-Turkish War
An estimated 25,000 supporters in a women's suffrage march on New York's Fifth Ave, led by Anna Howard Shaw andCarrie Chapman Catt, founder of the League of Women Voters
The Smurfs first appear in the story "Johan and Pirlouit" by Belgium cartoonistPeyo
Ignatius of Loyola, Spanish-Basque nobleman, priest, and co-founder of the Society of Jesus (Jesuit) religious order, born in Azpeitia, Spain (d. 1556)
